programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Despite the danger, he comes back years later, discovering that he's still a wanted man. But a kindhearted local named Chris (Dianne Foster) believes his story, and together they begin a desperate search for the true culprit.

In doing so, Pelias hopes for Jason's death to thwart the prophecy that he will reclaim the throne. Along the journey, Jason is protected by the goddess Hera (Honor Blackman), and his crew includes Hercules (Nigel Green) and Acastus (Gary Raymond), a saboteur planted by Pelias.

programme.desc: Dolores (Annette Funicello) and Frankie (Frankie Avalon), attractive young surfers in Southern California, are just out for a good time. programme.title: Man of the West

When he tries to intervene, he's knocked out and left in the middle of nowhere with saloon singer Billie (Julie London), whom he hoped to hire as a schoolteacher in his town, and Sam (Arthur O'Connell), a con man. Link brings the three to his nearby former home, now the hideout for the bandits, who are led by his uncle (Lee J. Cobb) -- and to save his new friends, he must rejoin the old gang for one last holdup.

programme.title: Renegades
programme.desc: Hannah Brockway has a perfect fiancé in Dr. Sam Martin. Sam, who is respected by Hannah's family, is a noble man who cares for her and can make a good living in their frontier town. Yet Hannah craves adventure and finds it when she meets dashing Ben Dembrow, the youngest of a notorious crime family. Together Ben and Hannah set off to forge a new life, but prejudice and strangling family ties soon bring trouble to these newlyweds.

programme.title: The Green Berets
programme.desc: A cynical reporter (David Janssen) who is opposed to the Vietnam War is sent to cover the conflict and assigned to tag along with a group of Green Berets. Led by the tough-as-nails Col. Mike Kirby (John Wayne), the team is given a top-secret mission to sneak behind enemy lines and kidnap an important Viet Cong commander. Along the way, the reporter learns to respect why America is involved in the war and helps to save the life of a war orphan whose life has been destroyed by the conflict.

programme.title: Betrayed
programme.desc: When the Dutch resistance brings in Carla Van Oven (Lana Turner) to spy on the Nazis, Col. Pieter Deventer (Clark Gable) initially suspects she could be a double agent. Van Oven eventually convinces Deventer of her character, and when she is sent into occupied territory, she joins up with a rebel leader known as "The Scarf" (Victor Mature). As the resistance endures severe losses and their missions fail, Deventer starts to wonder whose side Van Oven is fighting on.

programme.title: The Arizona Kid
programme.desc: Roy gallops into a skirmish with renegade soldiers who are terrorizing the Southwest during the Civil War.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Watson's encounter with Lady Frances Carfax brings Holmes rushing to the Lake District.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Rimfire
programme.desc: An undercover Army captain (James Millican) links missing gold and murder to a gambler's (Reed Hadley) ghost.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Dodge City and Fort Dodge get into a political war when the governor grants a special election to determine the new seat of Ford County; a conflicted Earp reluctantly follows Mayor Kelley's order to stop Fort Dodge from rigging the election.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: 2026 Proof Silver Eagle: America's 250th Anniversary Release
programme.desc: Join Mike Mezack for a historic release, the first-ever 1776-2026 dual-date Proof Silver Eagle with Liberty Bell privy mark, struck in .999 silver for America's 250th Anniversary; a one-year-only issue collectors won't want to miss.

programme.title: The Green Berets
programme.desc: A cynical reporter (David Janssen) who is opposed to the Vietnam War is sent to cover the conflict and assigned to tag along with a group of Green Berets. Led by the tough-as-nails Col. Mike Kirby (John Wayne), the team is given a top-secret mission to sneak behind enemy lines and kidnap an important Viet Cong commander. Along the way, the reporter learns to respect why America is involved in the war and helps to save the life of a war orphan whose life has been destroyed by the conflict.

programme.title: Betrayed
programme.desc: When the Dutch resistance brings in Carla Van Oven (Lana Turner) to spy on the Nazis, Col. Pieter Deventer (Clark Gable) initially suspects she could be a double agent. Van Oven eventually convinces Deventer of her character, and when she is sent into occupied territory, she joins up with a rebel leader known as "The Scarf" (Victor Mature). As the resistance endures severe losses and their missions fail, Deventer starts to wonder whose side Van Oven is fighting on.

programme.title: Ride the High Country
programme.desc: Reduced to transporting gold from a distant mine to a small-town bank, retired lawman Steve Judd (Joel McCrea) recruits friend Gil Westrum (Randolph Scott), who has been performing in a traveling carnival. Unknown to Steve, the restless Gil and a young drifter intend to steal the next gold transport. On the way, the men help Elsa Knudsen (Mariette Hartley) to break free from her zealot father and join her fiancé at the mine, not realizing the consequences that await them all.

programme.title: Renegades
programme.desc: Hannah Brockway has a perfect fiancé in Dr. Sam Martin. Sam, who is respected by Hannah's family, is a noble man who cares for her and can make a good living in their frontier town. Yet Hannah craves adventure and finds it when she meets dashing Ben Dembrow, the youngest of a notorious crime family. Together Ben and Hannah set off to forge a new life, but prejudice and strangling family ties soon bring trouble to these newlyweds.

programme.title: King of the Wild Horses
programme.desc: A mustang stallion runs swiftly to the rescue when the boy who befriended him is endangered.

programme.title: Life With Father
programme.desc: A New Yorker and her four sons experience love and laughter from their opinionated but well-meaning family patriarch.

programme.title: Bikini Beach
programme.desc: A shaggy British pop star (Frankie Avalon) woos a look-alike beach bum's girlfriend (Annette Funicello).

programme.title: Brewster's Millions
programme.desc: A returning GI (Dennis O'Keefe) inherits a fortune but must spend $1 million in 60 days to get it.

programme.title: A Dog's Life
programme.desc: Charlie rescues a stray from other dogs, and together they meet Edna after finding a wallet full of cash.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Maria Gibson arranges to meet her children's governess at Thor Bridge, but is gunned down. The young governess is accused of murder, which forces her employer to enlist the aid of Sherlock Holmes.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Stranger on Horseback
programme.desc: A circuit judge (Joel McCrea) rides in to break a ruthless cattle baron's stranglehold on a frontier town.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Dry Gulch Gang ambushes Earp's Cheyenne agents, killing Mr. Brother; a vengeful Earp stalks the men and confronts one when the killer comes to Dodge City to finish the job on a severely wounded Mr. Cousin.

programme.title: The Quick and the Dead
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger (Sam Elliott) befriends and protects a settler (Tom Conti) and his wife (Kate Capshaw) in 1876 Wyoming.

programme.title: Blazing Across the Pecos
programme.desc: The Durango Kid attempts to thwart an outlaw's plot to turn peaceful Indians against neighboring settlers.

programme.title: Good Day for a Hanging
programme.desc: The new marshal (Fred MacMurray) is the only one in town who thinks a charming killer (Robert Vaughn) should hang.

programme.title: The Desperadoes
programme.desc: An outlaw (Glenn Ford) blamed for a bank robbery helps a Utah sheriff (Randolph Scott) catch the real culprits.

programme.title: First Men in the Moon
programme.desc: An old man (Edward Judd) recalls how he, his fiancée (Martha Hyer) and a professor (Lionel Jeffries) planted a Union Jack on the moon in 1899.

programme.title: Conquest of Space
programme.desc: A captain (Walter Brooke), his son (Eric Fleming), a sergeant (Mickey Shaughnessy) and crew take off from a space station in a rocket ship for Mars.

programme.title: Avalanche
programme.desc: Hanging over a new ski resort are charges of corruption and a big snow wedge that's been there for years.

programme.title: Rockin' in the Rockies
programme.desc: Three buffoons prospect for gold and match wits with cattle rustlers while trying to break into Broadway.

programme.title: America's Heartland
programme.desc: Much of the food Americans eat is produced by farmers and ranchers in the country's heartland, but many people don't know how it's produced. "America's Heartland" provides information about the people and processes responsible for the availability of food and fuel across the country and around the world. The show's reporters and producers tell stories in topics that include farm families, consumer issues, animal welfare and crop sustainability. Some episodes also take the show abroad to countries such as Egypt and Taiwan to show the impact American agriculture has on the global economy.

programme.title: Bonanza
programme.desc: Ben Cartwright is the patriarch of an all-male Nevada ranching family. Set during and after the Civil War, "Bonanza" is the story of life on the family's thousand-acre spread, known as the Ponderosa, near Virginia City. Ben has his three sons, each with a different (and deceased) mother. Serious eldest son Adam hopes to follow in his father's footsteps; middle son Hoss is a gentle giant -- physically strong, but sometimes naive; and youngest son Little Joe is the most impetuous and romantic of the offspring.

programme.title: Weekend Recharge
programme.desc: Though it has "weekend" in the title, this show discusses the week ahead in addition to offering suggestions for what to do on Saturday and Sunday. Weather Channel meteorologists go beyond the elements outside and into the realm of lifestyle tips. Each host has a recurring segment on "Weekend Recharge," which includes helping parents get a fresh start to each day and explaining how the weather is expected to impact the weekend's scheduled sports action.

programme.desc: Jamie clears a snowed-in semi at the foot of the Rockies; Al fills a crucial role in a billion dollar infrastructure project; Ty and Andy wrestle a wreck to keep rush hour moving; Dylan uses the military rotator on an icy back road recovery.

programme.title: Rocky Mountain Wreckers
programme.desc: When winds blow a bus off of I-80, Art must wrestle it back; a load of produce rolls off, tearing down a fence that's keeping a herd of elk from the highway; Jesse races to finish a tough recovery in time to be part of an important family milestone.

programme.title: Rocky Mountain Wreckers
programme.desc: Charlie struggles with a surprise storm on Vail Pass; Brendan faces pressure after a promotion; Cory's rigging and patience are tested by a heavy manure load.

programme.desc: Starting the day by opening a window into weather, looking out at the stories and science behind the forecast.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Veteran salvor Paul Hay and his team rescue a clam-dredging crew's harvest lost in a storm off the New Jersey coast; following an annual cleanup operation in New Zealand that salvages fishing equipment gone astray.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Capt. John Ward and his crew are called to raise a sunken houseboat near Pensacola, Fla.; the owner of the boat wants it back, but the current occupiers of the boat living in it don't want John's services; there've been reports of gunfire too.

programme.title: Rough Cuts with Steven Rinella
programme.desc: Steve and Seth trap furbearers while discussing the history, merits and challenges of this ancient tradition.

programme.title: Fishing the Wild West
programme.desc: Fly-fishing Battle Creek and Loch Leven with a journey into the wild west at Reesor Ranch.

programme.title: Fishing 411
programme.desc: Host and producer Mark Romanack shares his 30-plus years of professional fishing savvy with viewers.

programme.title: Fishing the Flats
programme.desc: Featuring top celebrity fish catches!

programme.title: Wild Game Cooking With Chef Rupert
programme.desc: Chef Rupert shows how to prepare a pan roasted sous vide Cornish hen with sautéed ramps and potato lyonnaise.

programme.title: Shooting USA
programme.desc: Reporter Jim Scoutten covers what fans and consumers wish to know about the shooting sports industry, including reports from national championships, field tests of product performance claims, history lessons from gun collectors, tips from professional marksmen, along with demonstrations from the world's top exhibition shooters. Also check out industry's newest inventory being subjected to review processes.

programme.title: The Obsession of Carter Andrews
programme.desc: Carter heads to the west coast of Florida to tag and release goliath grouper in the name of research, but the tackle of choice may call for a more hands-on approach that makes staying aboard the boat a challenge.

programme.title: Crush With Lee & Tiffany
programme.desc: Lee heads to Texas to meet up with Brendan Burns for his first ever Axis Deer hunt and Tiffany notches her Iowa turkey tag.

programme.title: Wardens: Case Files
programme.desc: Witness how an unlucky poacher hops in the wrong boat; an unlikely species makes its way onto a warden's radar.

programme.title: MLF General Tire Team Series
programme.desc: Major League Fishing's General Tire Team Series is a new format in professional bass fishing where 27 teams have the ability to communicate and share information with each other as they compete for a prize.

programme.title: Canada in the Rough
programme.desc: Paul Beasley and his father, Ken, are on a true moose hunting adventure in Northwestern Ontario.

programme.title: Canada in the Rough
programme.desc: Kevin Beasley is hunting pronghorn with owner of Ridgetec Game Cameras, Chase Wendorff, in Southern Alberta.

programme.title: Angler & Hunter TV
programme.desc: Outdoor fishing and hunting action with host Mike Miller.

programme.title: Guns & Ammo TV
programme.desc: The Aimpoint Duty RDS MR optic.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher
programme.desc: From basics to advanced techniques, experts and guests offer novice and experienced anglers advice for catching pike, trout, salmon, bass and other fresh- and salt-water fish native to North America. Episodes include underwater footage, animated sequences and aerial shots of North American landscapes and wildlife. The series travels to U.S. locations in Wyoming and Niagara Falls, N.Y., as well as the Canadian provinces of Ontario, Alberta and Quebec.

programme.title: Gun Stories
programme.desc: Select fire handguns might have limited capacity and application but are still exhilarating to shoot. Join host, Joe Mantegna as he looks back at some of the most exciting and interesting full auto pistols in firearms history.

programme.title: Fishing the Rock
programme.desc: Mackerel.

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Shock Therapy
programme.desc: Jim and Lionel share several gripping near-death experiences, including the harrowing time Lionel was kidnapped by guerrillas in Africa and the life-or-death moments that followed.

programme.title: MOJO TV
programme.desc: Innovative designs for new hunting products.

programme.title: Young Wild Hunters
programme.desc: Mountain Roe Deer Hunt.

programme.title: Descendants of the North
programme.desc: Jeff Coyle shares his adventurous and educational journey into the wild.

programme.title: Driven With Pat & Nicole
programme.desc: Pat, Nicole, and Cash hunt spring bear in Northern Saskatchewan. Pat hunts a giant nocturnal red bear he's been after for years!

programme.title: Chassomaniak Next Generation
programme.desc: Hunting trip in Africa.

programme.title: Ranch America
programme.desc: River joins up with cowboy Jeff Mundell for a working tour of the historic Gabilan Ranch.

programme.title: Gary Cooper's Fishing Diary
programme.desc: Mike Denski lands the largest halibut of his life.

programme.title: Doubled Down Outdoors
programme.desc: Follow along as host Ryan Tomuschat chases big Alberta moose with his bow.

programme.title: Trev Gowdy's Monsterfish
programme.desc: In search of monster Kingfish off the coast of Florida. Will Fred's kite technique work? Kingfish are known to leap upwards of twenty feet in the air!

programme.title: The Gamekeepers of Mossy Oak
programme.desc: This week, Dr. Marcus Lashley of the University of Florida joins us to explain his team's ongoing wild turkey poult research. It's fascinating stuff. Listen to the full conversation on Gamekeeper Podcast #332.

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Hunting Adventures
programme.desc: Jim Shockey has tracked game in more than 40 countries and taken in excess of 300 species using bow, muzzleloader and rifle; he has exclusive guiding territories on Vancouver Island and in his native Saskatchewan.

programme.title: Elk Camp
programme.desc: Renowned elk caller and professional guide Steve Chappell guides his clients on up close, bugling elk hunts in Arizona and Colorado; Steve's calling skills and capacity for understanding elk behavior are on display each week.

programme.title: Radical Restoration
programme.desc: The crew begins work on a 1969 Charger that turns out to be a surprise birthday present.

programme.title: The Next Bite
programme.desc: Tommy Kemos is joined by a crew of heavy hitters for a great lakes ice beatdown!

programme.desc: (1955) Cold War-influenced western starring Glenn Ford and Edward G Robinson. A smallholder stands alone against the expansionist plans and brutal tactics of a tyrannical rancher.

programme.title: The Man from Laramie
programme.desc: (1955) Classic western. James Stewart plays a stranger who arrives in a remote New Mexico town secretly seeking his brother's killer. With Donald Crisp, Alex Nicol and Arthur Kennedy.

programme.title: Save Yourselves!
programme.desc: (2020) Gentle apocalypse comedy with Sunita Mani and John Reynolds. An NYC hipster couple do digital detox in the sticks. But furry aliens invade while they're off-grid. Unfortunate timing.

programme.title: Big Trouble in Little China
programme.desc: (1986) John Carpenter's tongue-in-cheek martial-arts romp starring Kurt Russell and Kim Cattrall. Can a truck driver rescue his girlfriend from a sorcerer before she loses her eyes?

programme.title: Bone Tomahawk
programme.desc: (2016) Wild West horror starring Kurt Russell, Lili Simmons, Patrick Wilson, Richard Jenkins and Matthew Fox. Can a posse save townsfolk from their cannibalistic abductors?

programme.title: Friends with Benefits
programme.desc: (2011) Romcom. Justin Timberlake and Mila Kunis star as friends who aren't fans of romance. But they do like sex. Can they really sleep together but avoid all that emotion?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: How Green Was My Valley
programme.desc: (1941) Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara and Roddy McDowall star in John Ford's multi-Oscar-winning period drama set in the mining communities of the South Wales Valleys of the 19th century.

programme.title: Mary, Queen of Scots
programme.desc: (1971) Vanessa Redgrave stars in a dramatisation of the life of the 16th-century Scottish queen. Glenda Jackson co-stars as her cousin and rival, England's Queen Elizabeth I.

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes:...
programme.desc: ...Talking Film: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections.

programme.title: Warlock
programme.desc: (1959) Western starring Henry Fonda. A gunfighter is hired to protect a town from a raiding gang. But a vendetta could undermine him. With Anthony Quinn, Richard Widmark and Dorothy Malone.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead...
programme.desc: ...Reckoning Part 1 Interview Special: Tom Cruise, Hayley Atwell, Simon Pegg and director Christopher McQuarrie talk about the spectacular seventh film in the action-adventure series.

programme.title: Deep Impact
programme.desc: (1998) Elijah Wood and Morgan Freeman star in the sci-fi disaster movie about impending meteorite-related disaster, attempts to destroy it, and possibly some cataclysmic havoc.

programme.title: Peppermint
programme.desc: (2018) Revenge thriller with Jennifer Garner. A mum turns vigilante on a quest for justice when her husband and daughter are murdered by a drug cartel.

programme.title: Daredevil
programme.desc: (2003) Superhero fantasy. Ben Affleck is a blind lawyer who turns vigilante by night. Jennifer Garner plays his lover and Colin Farrell the villain who endangers them both. Strong violence.

programme.title: Call Jane
programme.desc: (2022) Fact-based drama with Elizabeth Banks and Sigourney Weaver. An underground abortion network helps a woman with a life-threatening pregnancy in pre-Roe-v-Wade America.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: All the King's Men
programme.desc: (1949) Drama with John Ireland. A local politician begins to lust for the kind of power he used to hold to account. He should be careful how he treats people on the way to the top...

programme.title: Tomahawk
programme.desc: (1951) Western starring Van Heflin. During the uprising of the Sioux nation, a cunning scout attempts to save the 7th Cavalry after an officer orders the brutal massacre of Sioux innocents.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: (1971) Film version of the popular comedy series set in 1940. The Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard, led by Arthur Lowe's Captain Mainwaring, bumble through a series of misadventures.

programme.title: The Bravados
programme.desc: (1958) Brooding western starring Joan Collins and Gregory Peck. A man hunts down the four escaped prisoners who he believes are responsible for the murder of his wife.

programme.title: Fisherman's Friends
programme.desc: (2019) Heart-warming comedy based on the real-life story of the Cornish folk ensemble who took the UK charts by storm in 2010, starring Daniel Mays and Tuppence Middleton.

programme.title: The Equalizer
programme.desc: (2014) Thriller. Denzel Washington stars as an ex-soldier who comes out of retirement to help a teenage sex worker who's been assaulted by her pimp.

programme.title: Sisu
programme.desc: (2022) Jalmari Helander's ultra-violent, Nazi-slaying survival adventure starring Jorma Tommila. A bunch of Nazis try to steal a grizzled Finnish ex-soldier's gold. But he ain't having it.

programme.title: The Last Tree
programme.desc: (2019) Shola Amoo's semi-autobiographical coming-of-age drama follows a young boy's journey from idyllic Lincolnshire countryside to a London high-rise.

programme.title: Justice With Judge Mablean
programme.desc: Judge Mablean Ephriam, who presided over "Divorce Court" from 1999-2006 as the first star of the revived version of the show, returns to the courtroom genre with his half-hour series that deals with life and the law. The former Los Angeles-based prosecutor takes on the typical cases that are found on TV court shows. The arbitrator says that her show "will be life because everything we do, it involves the law."

Former judge and prosecutor Kevin Ross adjudicates real cases, in which he tries to show litigants alternative ways of dealing with their issues and make them consider the consequences of their actions.

programme.title: The Verdict With Judge Hatchett
programme.desc: Judge Glenda Hatchett presided over her self-titled courtroom show from 2000-2008. Now, she returns to the medium with "The Verdict." In the half-hour series, the Atlanta native and former juvenile-court judge rules over small-claims cases brought in front of her. Hatchett's courtroom isn't just about finding someone guilty, though. She also uses her experience dealing with young people and her tough-love approach to help send a message to out-of-control juvenile offenders to try to set them on the right track in their lives.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: Karen Mills was a county court judge and criminal defense attorney in Florida before turning to TV to preside over her own court shows. In her third effort, "Supreme Justice," Mills tackles small-claims court arbitrations in her compassionate way, to which viewers of her previous shows have grown accustomed. As usual with court shows, cases involve friendships gone bad, money disputes and property damage.
